role_specializations.summary: Architect specialization for read-only topology, owner, and boundary stewardship.
activation_triggers: A source tree shape or owner boundary is unclear.
A sibling repository pattern needs interpretation before local adoption.
Generated reader authority may be drifting above source authority.
projection_notes: May project as architect-topology-steward, but source remains inside the architect role house.
Projection should be read-only unless paired with a separate workspace-write specialization.
stop_lines: Do not mutate source state through this specialization.
Do not turn topology analysis into proof doctrine or durable memory canon.

role_specializations.summary: Coder specialization for bounded repo-local refactors with generated-reader repair.
activation_triggers: A bounded repo-local structure change has an explicit owner surface.
Generated readers need regeneration from changed source objects.
Validators or tests need to move with the new source topology.
projection_notes: May project as coder-repo-refactor, but source remains inside the coder role house.
Projection must keep mutation authority repo-local unless a stronger runtime route is explicit.
stop_lines: Do not mutate service, runtime, secret, plugin, hook, or network posture through this specialization.
Do not hand-edit generated readers when the source builder can regenerate them.

role_specializations.summary: Reviewer specialization for read-only drift, stale-name, and route-boundary inspection.
activation_triggers: Legacy source names may still be active noise.
A generated reader may point at a stale source path.
A change needs review for route-law drift before landing.
projection_notes: May project as reviewer-route-drift-review, but source remains inside the reviewer role house.
Projection should be read-only and findings-first.
stop_lines: Do not land fixes through this specialization.
Do not treat a review finding as durable memory canon.

role_specializations.summary: Evaluator specialization for read-only release readiness and evidence sufficiency.
activation_triggers: A change claims to be ready for release or merge.
Validators, generated readers, and tests must be weighed together.
A reviewer needs bounded evaluation rather than implementation.
projection_notes: May project as evaluator-release-readiness, but source remains inside the evaluator role house.
Projection should expose evidence and validation commands, not write authority.
stop_lines: Do not mutate source state through this specialization.
Do not promote readiness judgment into eval doctrine.

role_specializations.summary: Memory-keeper specialization for writeback checks and local memory candidates.
activation_triggers: A meaningful topology, route-law, or workflow change needs writeback judgment.
Session evidence must be preserved as a candidate rather than treated as canon.
A closeout needs explicit memory-worthy or no-writeback reasoning.
projection_notes: May project as memory-keeper-writeback-steward, but source remains inside the memory-keeper role house.
Projection may prepare candidates but must not grant durable promotion rights by default.
stop_lines: Do not promote, demote, retire, or rewrite durable memory truth through this specialization.
Do not write outside a repo-local memory candidate route.
